summary: 10 concrete, comment-endorsed recommendations selected from a verified complete HN front-page comment crawl.
telegram_footer: 10 items surfaced | 30/30 current front-page stories processed | history and run artifact updated.

items.name: Bento
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Bento - An entire PowerPoint in one HTML file (edit+view+data+collab)
items.summary: A self-contained HTML presentation editor with offline use and optional encrypted collaboration.
items.why_included: Exceptional practical enthusiasm for a portable, editable presentation file made this stand out.
items.evidence: A commenter called it ‘really clever,’ praised the self-contained direction, and said it was worth using soon.

items.name: Nash
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Bento - An entire PowerPoint in one HTML file (edit+view+data+collab)
items.summary: A standalone HTML note-taking app intended as a lighter alternative to TiddlyWiki.
items.why_included: A concrete personal recommendation for people overwhelmed by heavier personal-wiki software.
items.evidence: A commenter explicitly recommended Nash for people who feel overwhelmed by TiddlyWiki.

items.name: GigaToken
items.summary: A high-throughput tokenizer targeting model training pipelines and lower inference time-to-first-token.
items.why_included: Independent tokenization researchers singled out its cache and pretokenization work as broadly useful.
items.evidence: A practitioner called it ‘fantastic work,’ saying the tokenization community wanted to absorb its lessons.

items.name: Natural Number Game
items.source_story_title: Introduction to Formal Verification with Lean Part 1
items.summary: An interactive Lean course that builds natural-number proofs from the Peano axioms.
items.why_included: It is a notably approachable on-ramp to Lean rather than another passive reference.
items.evidence: Multiple commenters called it ‘amazing’ and ‘the best intro to Lean,’ strongly recommending it to beginners.

items.name: pgBackRest
items.summary: A PostgreSQL backup and restore tool with point-in-time recovery and efficient incremental backups.
items.why_included: Several experienced operators independently preferred it over hand-rolled backup scripts.
items.evidence: One team reported easy setup and reliable point-in-time recovery; another said it saved substantial storage cost.

items.name: wide
items.summary: A Rust crate that makes common SIMD operations more accessible than manual intrinsics.
items.why_included: It is a small, directly actionable recommendation from real high-performance code work.
items.evidence: A bioinformatics developer reported AVX-512 gains and said wide makes common operations ‘completely trivial.’

items.name: fearless_simd
items.summary: A Rust SIMD library from the Linebender ecosystem for writing vectorized code.
items.why_included: It was specifically identified as the starting package for Rust developers adding SIMD.
items.evidence: A commenter recommended it directly as the package to start with for Rust SIMD.

items.name: Marc's Apollo Guidance Computer restoration videos
items.source_story_title: Original Apollo 11 Guidance Computer source code for command and lunar modules
items.summary: A video series documenting the restoration and operation of the Apollo Guidance Computer.
items.why_included: A deep technical historical resource, recommended specifically as an exceptional AGC explainer.
items.evidence: A commenter said they could not recommend Marc’s restoration videos enough and called the work incredible.

items.name: ScreenWall
items.source_story_title: ScreenWall – Turn old phones into synced widgets for your space
items.summary: A browser-based system for turning old phones into synchronized widgets and display walls.
items.why_included: It solves a concrete reuse problem with unusually broad home-dashboard and installation potential.
items.evidence: A commenter called it ‘cool and useful,’ while others highlighted home dashboards, flight simulators, and e-readers.

items.name: Radio Paradise
items.source_story_title: It's a shame what's happened to radio
items.summary: A listener-supported internet radio station focused on eclectic, human-curated music discovery.
items.why_included: A long-running listener recommendation offers a practical answer to the thread’s search for good radio.
items.evidence: A commenter said it had been their go-to for years and that they had discovered much great music through it.
